message:
refactor(core): unify column-name inference on select_result_column_names + clippy cleanup pass

Two related cleanups: (1) finish migrating every column-name
inference site in fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs over to the
unified self.select_result_column_names() helper, including the
final hold-out — build_join_col_map / col_map_for_source — and
delete the now-unreferenced infer_select_column_names() free
function plus its CTE-aware twin; and (2) clear the clippy lint
backlog that had accumulated across the recent
collation/quiescence/profiling commits in this same batch.

== Column-name inference unification ==

crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs

- Top-of-file `#![allow(...)]` block: add
  `clippy::mutable_key_type` with an explanatory comment.
  SmallText carries a OnceLock for lazy long-string interning,
  which trips mutable_key_type on every HashMap<SmallText, _>
  site even though Hash and Eq remain stable.  The interior
  mutability is single-write initialization only, never
  observable through the trait impls, so the lint is a false
  positive across the whole file.

- VIEW execution path (~line 25198): drop the
  `infer_select_column_names() + resolve_cte_column_names_with_schema()`
  fallback dance and call self.select_result_column_names(
      &view.query, &[], &mut Vec::new()
  ) instead.  Same call shape that's now used everywhere else.

- build_join_col_map(&self, select):
    - Was: snapshot self.schema.borrow() and call the static
      `Self::col_map_for_source(source, &schema, col_map)`
      twice — once for from.source and once per join.table.
    - Now: collect the SELECT's WITH-bound CTEs into a
      `let mut visible_ctes = Vec::new();` (cloned from
      select.with.ctes when present) and call the *instance*
      method `self.col_map_for_source(source, &visible_ctes,
      col_map)`.  Threading visible_ctes through means a
      JOIN whose right-hand side references a CTE bound by an
      enclosing WITH gets the right column names instead of
      falling back to the schema.

- col_map_for_source: rewritten as an instance method that
  takes `&self`, `source`, `&[fsqlite_ast::Cte]`, and the
  output col_map.  The body collapses from a 75-line
  match-on-source-then-match-on-select-body cascade into a
  thin dispatch:
    Table { name, alias }    → schema lookup as before, but
        also check visible_ctes first so a CTE alias shadows
        a real table of the same name.
    Subquery { query, alias } → call
        self.select_result_column_names(query, visible_ctes,
        &mut Vec::new()) and emit (label, col_name, false) for
        each result.
  Net deletion: ~75 lines of duplicated SelectCore walking,
  GROUP BY-vs-DISTINCT branching, and ad hoc star expansion
  that was already living inside select_result_column_names.

- INSERT-from-SELECT and CREATE TABLE AS SELECT helpers
  (~line 34602): drop the same `infer_select_column_names`
  + schema fallback duo at three more call sites; all now
  flow through self.select_result_column_names.

- delete `fn infer_select_column_names(select: &SelectStatement)
  -> Vec<String>` and its companion
  `fn resolve_cte_column_names_with_schema(...)` from the
  module entirely.  The grep over the workspace confirms no
  other callers, so they leave no dangling references.

- New regression test (~line 99162) covers the JOIN-against-
  CTE case that the unified path now handles correctly:
    test_build_join_col_map_resolves_cte_aliases_in_visible_with
  builds
      WITH cte AS (SELECT 1 AS id, 'left' AS payload)
      SELECT t.id, t.payload, r.tag FROM cte AS t JOIN rhs r ON r.id = t.id
  and asserts col_map contains
      ("t", "id"), ("t", "payload"), ("r", "id"), ("r", "tag")
  in order, with no schema lookup needed for "t".

== Clippy cleanup pass ==

crates/fsqlite-pager/src/page_cache.rs
- PageCache::Debug impl: replace `.finish()` with
  `.finish_non_exhaustive()` so the formatter does not pretend
  the printed fields are an exhaustive enumeration.  Pure cosmetic;
  silences clippy::missing_fields_in_debug indirectly.

crates/fsqlite-planner/src/differential.rs
- DifferentialOutput::explain_label: switch the `alias` parameter
  passed to render_output_label from `&Option<String>` to
  `alias.as_ref()` (Option<&String>) so the helper can stop
  taking a reference-to-Option (clippy::ref_option_ref).
- DifferentialPlanError::Display: collapse the six identical
  `Self::Unsupported* { detail } => write!(f, "{detail}")`
  arms into a single multi-arm pattern using `|` (clippy::
  match_same_arms).
- render_output_label: signature flips to
  `alias: Option<&String>` for the same reason.  All call sites
  pass alias.as_ref().

crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs
- Two pre-existing clippy nits surfaced by recent edits to the
  surrounding test scaffolding:
    - SqliteValue::Float construction in the test fixture now
      uses `(seed as f64).mul_add(1.625, -777.25)` instead of
      the multiply-then-subtract pair (clippy::suboptimal_flops).
    - rusqlite oracle row materialization: drop the
      `Ok(...)? + collect::<rusqlite::Result<Vec<_>>>()?` shape
      and let the closure return the inner Result directly
      (clippy::needless_question_mark).

crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/lib.rs
- enter_vdbe_decode_profile_stage / _execute_ / _commit_:
  drop the `#[must_use]` attribute.  All three callers
  immediately bind the marker into a `_pipeline_profile_stage`
  variable whose Drop is the meaningful side effect, so the
  attribute was producing noise without preventing any real
  bug.
- compute_requires_attached_memdb: collapse the nested
  `match Opcode { Opcode::IdxGE | ... | Opcode::FusedAppendInsert => {
        if !storage_cursor_ids.contains(&op.p1) { return true; }
   } }` into a single arm with an `if !storage_cursor_ids.contains(...)`
  guard (clippy::collapsible_if + clippy::collapsible_match).

crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/make_record_simd.rs
- classify_integer_block_avx2: add
  `#[allow(clippy::cast_ptr_alignment)]` with an inline
  comment "_mm256_*u_si256 are unaligned-safe intrinsics".
  The lint is correct in general but wrong here because the
  loadu/storeu intrinsics explicitly accept misaligned
  pointers; a `mem::align_of::<__m256i>() == 32` check would
  reject the call site even though it's safe by spec.
- try_serialize_integer_record_iter_into: visibility upgraded
  from `pub(crate)` to `pub` so the new VDBE pipeline_stages
  bench (added earlier in this batch) can call it from
  outside the crate root without re-exporting wrappers.

crates/fsqlite-vfs/tests/bd_3u7_4_vfs_contract_io_uring_faults.rs
- Add #[allow(clippy::struct_field_names)] above
  InjectedFaultState — the struct legitimately has fields
  named next_read / next_write / next_sync because each
  represents the next-injected fault for that operation kind,
  and the lint's "field names should not repeat the struct
  name" heuristic doesn't really apply.

No behavior changes anywhere outside the column-name inference
unification (which itself is a bug fix already exercised by the
existing JOIN-with-CTE tests landed in the earlier commit
"fix(core): JOIN source subquery column resolution via
select_result_column_names + remove test diagnostics").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
